g.text: IAM Binding — attached to the Service Account

text.tspan: Validates the incoming token (from Github) against the pool and provider config.
If valid, exchanges it for a short-lived federated token
that can then be used to request service account impersonation.
Identity
github-actions-sa@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com
GCP Resource Role
roles/artifactregistry.writer
This is the role that grants access to GCP resources — in this example,
Artifact Registry Writer allows pushing Docker images to Artifact Registry.
Swap this for whatever role matches the access your external system needs.
Role
roles/iam.workloadIdentityUser
Grants the right to impersonate this service account.
Does not grant access to any GCP resource on its own.
Member (principal)
principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/PROJECT_NUMBER/locations/global
/workloadIdentityPools/POOL_ID/attribute.repository/my-org/my-repo
Scoped to a specific attribute from the token.
Only WIF identities whose token matches attribute.repository == my-org/my-repo
are allowed to impersonate this service account.
Tokens that don't match are rejected before reaching the service account.

g.text: shares the validation method and passing criteria
attached to — checks every impersonation request
